data.title: BioCryst Pharmaceuticals reports prelim 2025 ORLADEYO revenue, provides 2026 guidance
data.description:
• BioCryst Pharmaceuticals (BCRX) reported preliminary, unaudited Q4 2025 ORLADEYO net revenue of $151M, up 22% Y/Y.
  • Full-year 2025 ORLADEYO revenue reached $601M, a 37% increase Y/Y.
  • BioCryst expects 2026 global ORLADEYO revenue of $625M–645M and total revenue, including RAPIVAB®, of $635M–660M.
  • Year-end 2025 cash, cash equivalents, restricted cash, and investments totaled ~$338M.
  • Astria acquisition, expected to close in Q1 2026, will add $70M–80M in additional operating expenses to support Phase 3 enrollment of navenibart and commercial readiness.

data.title: Synchrony expands CareCredit to health and wellness providers through the Clover app
data.description:
• Synchrony (SYF) on Monday announced an expansion of its integration with Clover, the all-in-one commerce solution from Fiserv (FISV).
  • The partnership expands CareCredit's network with an additional 40,000 health providers, in addition to the 2,000 health & wellness providers already accepting CareCredit payments with Clover.
  • Synchrony (SYF) fell 9%, tracking the broader market decline.

data.title: Silver spikes as safe-haven demand jumps on Powell investigation
data.description:
Silver (XAGUSD:CUR) spiked higher on Monday morning climbing to yet another all-time trading high after reports that the U.S. attorney’s office has opened a criminal investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell.

To start the trading week, investors watched silver surge sharply, jumping about 6% in early Monday trading and briefly climbing as high as 7.3% at its peak. 

Silver’s upward momentum has carried into 2026, showing little sign of easing after a standout performance in 2025, when the precious metal surged by roughly 150% for the year. Less than a month into the new year, silver has already added another 18.4%, extending its year-to-date gains and underscoring the strength of the ongoing rally. 

Speaking on the behalf of silver, Seeking Alpha analyst David Desjardins said: “It is my personal view that silver is currently in the midst of a generational bull market underpinned by structural industrial demand growth.” 

For investors looking to track the price of silver they can look to monitor both silver and silver mining exchange trade funds. Outlined below are a group of related funds: 

Silver ETFs: (SLV), (SIVR), (AGQ), (ZSL), and (PSLV). 

Silver Mining ETFs: (SIL), (SILJ), (SLVP), (SHNY), and (SLJY).

data.title: Sekur Private Data launches $1.05M non-brokered private placement
data.description:
• Sekur Private Data (SWISF) announced a non-brokered private placement to raise up to CA$1.5M (US$1.05M) through the issuance of up to 30M units.
  • Each unit consists of one common share at CA$0.05 and one full warrant, exercisable at CA$0.10 per share for 48 months.
  • Net proceeds are intended to fund sales, business development, and general working capital.

data.title: Burford Capital plans $450M senior notes offering to redeem 2026 bonds
data.description:
• Burford Capital (BUR) announced a planned private offering of $450M senior notes due 2034 through its wholly owned subsidiary, Burford Capital Global Finance.
  • The notes will be senior unsecured and guaranteed by Burford Capital.
  • Net proceeds are intended to redeem Burford Capital PLC’s 5.000% bonds due 2026 and to fund general corporate purposes, including potential repayment of other indebtedness.
  • Upon completion of the redemption, Burford Capital Finance LLC and Burford Capital PLC are expected to be released from guarantees on other outstanding debt.

data.title: Trump to interview Rick Rieder for Fed chair this week - report
data.description:
President Donald Trump will interview Rick Rieder, BlackRock's (BLK), chief investment officer of Global Fixed Income, for the Federal Reserve chair job, the last of his meetings with candidates for the role, according to a media report on Monday.

The president will interview Rick Rieder Thursday at the White House, Fox Business reported, citing administration sources. In addition to the president and Rieder, Chief of Staff Susie Wiles, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, Deputy Chief of Staff Dan Scavino will attend the meeting, the report said.

Trump has previously said he has already decided on this Fed chair nominee and would name that person sometime in January. In a New York Times interview, he said, "I have in my mind a decision," but he declined to name the person.

The four finalists are Rieder,  National Economic Council head Kevin Hassett, former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh, and Fed Governor Christopher Waller.

The interviews are taking place as the president pressures the Fed to lower interest rates to make it cheaper for businesses, and the government, to borrow money. The lower rates would boost economic activity and reduce the interest that the government will pay on future bonds.

Rieder recently issued a note calling for at least two more policy rate cuts in 2026, which would bring the federal funds rate target range to 3.00%-3.25% from its current 3.50%-3.75%.

Over the weekend, the Department of Justice launched an investigation of Fed Chair Jerome Powell over the central bank's renovation of its headquarters and whether he lied to Congress about the project's scope. While Powell has tried to remain quiet on Trump's criticisms of him, Powell issued a statement saying the investigation is over Trump's disagreement with the central bank's rate policy.

In prediction markets, contracts have swung between Hassett and Warsh as favorites to succeed Powell. On Polymarket, Hassett is currently the leader with a 45% chance of getting the job, followed by Warsh with a 40% chance. The odds on Waller are 8% and on Rieder 6%.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: H&R REIT declares CAD 0.05 dividend
data.description:
• H&R REIT (HRUFF) declares CAD 0.05/share monthly dividend.
  • Payable Feb. 17; for shareholders of record Jan. 30; ex-div Jan. 30.

data.title: Myriad Genetics Q4 and 2025 revenue ranges include consensus
data.description:
• Myriad Genetics' (MYGN) Q4 and 2025 revenue ranges include the consensus figure.
  • Q4 revenue is expected at $207M-$209M. Consensus is $207.13M. Full-year revenue is seen at $822M-$824M. Consensus is $821.77M.
  • Myriad is schedule to report Q4 and 2025 financial results on Feb. 24.

data.title: Planet Fitness gains after updating on key year-end metrics
data.description:
Planet Fitness (PLNT) provided a business update ahead of the company's appearance at the ICR Conference in Orlando, Florida.

The gym club operator reported that it grew its membership to approximately 20.8 million members, and full-year system-wide same-club sales increased by 6.7%. Planet Fitness (PLNT) also noted that it placed equipment in 152 new franchised locations and opened 181 new Planet Fitness clubs in 2025, including 23 corporate-owned locations, bringing the system-wide total clubs count to 2,896.

CEO update: "We delivered strong results in 2025 and exceeded key growth objectives we outlined at the start of the year. We continue to capture even greater opportunities in the evolving global fitness landscape and deliver value to our stakeholders. Key achievements included our franchisees proactively increasing the strength equipment in their clubs to enhance the member experience; signing up a record number of participants in our High School Summer Pass Program as we continue to resonate with younger consumers; and reaching an agreement with our franchisees to shift a portion of contributions from the Local Ad Fund to the National Ad Fund for 2026 to optimize and accelerate new marketing opportunities and drive future member growth."

Looking ahead, Planet Fitness (PLNT) sees itself as well-positioned to meet the increasing demand for fitness with a "judgment-free, high-quality, and affordable experience" as consumers prioritize their health and well-being.

Shares of Planet Fitness (PLNT) were up 2.1% in premarket trading to $109.14 vs. the 52-week range of $87.72 to $114.47.

data.title: KORU Medical Systems sees Q4 revenue of $10.9M
data.description:
• KORU Medical Systems (KRMD) on Monday said preliminary, unaudited fourth quarter 2025 net revenues expected to be $10.9 million, representing growth of 23% over the prior year period.
  • Preliminary, unaudited FY25 net revenues expected to be $41.1 million, representing growth of 22% over the prior year.
  • Consensus for FY25 and Q4 revenues are $40.69M and $10.46M.
  • Ending cash balance of $8.9 million, representing positive cash flow in the fourth quarter and positive cash flow from operations for the full year.
  • KRMD -3.89% premarket to $5.43.
  • Source: Press Release
